title: ozanonia dinarica
intro: Ozanonia dinarica, also known as the Montenegro's oak fern, is a species of fern native to the Dinaric Alps in the Balkan Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ozanonia dinarica include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve overall lung function. Herbal preparations of Ozanonia dinarica typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and stems, which can be consumed as a tea or used in combination with other herbs to create a syrup or tincture.
